target.title: docs: add Plugin Inspector development and CI workflow
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a canonical Plugin Inspector guide for plugin-author development, CI, trusted runtime capture, beta-checkout comparison, reports, and plugin-shipped skill proof.
- Reorder the plugin testing guide so external authors see package, runtime, and live-test guidance before OpenClaw's repo-only test helper reference.
- Link the workflow from the plugin quickstart and tool-plugin CI guide, and replace the stale future-tense Inspector section in the compatibility reference.
- Keep the full Plugin Inspector command/API reference in its owner repository; this PR documents how it fits into OpenClaw plugin development.

## Linked context

Related: https://github.com/openclaw/clawhub/pull/2627

Requested during the ClawHub/OpenClaw plugin documentation consolidation work.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: OpenClaw's canonical docs did not explain how external plugin authors should use Plugin Inspector during development and CI, while the testing page led with repo-only helpers and the compatibility page described the Inspector as future work.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw docs checkout at `2ab6caeb80` and the published `@openclaw/plugin-inspector@0.3.15` CLI.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `npx --yes @openclaw/plugin-inspector@0.3.15 --help`; `pnpm docs:list`; `pnpm format:docs:check`; `pnpm docs:check-mdx`; `pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary` with the new page included; `pnpm lint:docs`; `pnpm docs:check-links`; `git diff --check`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): `pnpm docs:list` lists `plugins/plugin-inspector.md`; the current Inspector CLI help confirms the documented `check`, `inspect`, `ci`, `config`, `init`, checkout, runtime, author-facing, SARIF, and JUnit surfaces; all listed repository docs checks pass.
- Observed result after fix: the new page is discoverable in navigation and passes OpenClaw's formatting, MDX, glossary, lint, JSON, and internal-link checks.
- What was not tested: a completed local Mintlify render and Mintlify anchor audit.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the local Mintlify preview and `mint broken-links --check-anchors` stop on existing parse errors in unrelated current-main pages under `docs/plugins/reference/`; the pnpm anchor runner also hits an isolated Mintlify `react` peer-resolution error.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): `docs/plugins/compatibility.md` used a future-tense day-one CLI and stale `@openclaw/plugin-inspector@0.1.0` examples; `docs/plugins/sdk-testing.md` started with repo-only bundled-plugin helpers.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm format:docs:check`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
- `pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary` with the new page included through a temporary index
- `pnpm lint:docs`
- `pnpm docs:check-links`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `git diff --check`
- `git merge-tree --write-tree upstream/main HEAD`

No runtime test was added because this is a docs-only change.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`, documentation only.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`.

What is the highest-risk area? Inaccurate or unsafe Plugin Inspector command guidance.

How is that risk mitigated? The workflow is grounded in the current published Inspector README, package metadata, CLI help, and CLI source. Runtime capture is explicitly separated and limited to trusted code.

## Current review state

Ready for maintainer review and CI. No known author-side changes remain.
target.author: fuller-stack-dev
